evidenceScope: general spider fallback
typicalOutcome: General fallback used when no supported species, genus, or family estimate is available.

sources.label: Prospective study of 750 definite spider bites

evidenceScope: small Oregon multi-genus verified-bite series
typicalOutcome: Localized pain, redness, itching, or swelling; occasional minor systemic symptoms were reported. No dermonecrosis or medically significant envenoming occurred in the qualifying Oregon series.
calculationMethod: provisional case-pattern estimate from verified bites spanning Agelenopsis, Hololena, and Eratigena

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort estimate
typicalOutcome: Usually localized pain and skin effects; severe pain and minor systemic symptoms occurred in minorities of the qualifying Australian orb-weaver cohort.
calculationMethod: cohort-table approximation: 2.5 + 2 × max(severe-pain rate, systemic-effects rate); the maximum is used because overlap was not reported

sources.label: Prospective study of 750 definite spider bites

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort estimate
typicalOutcome: Predominantly localized effects in the small qualifying Australian cohort; minor systemic symptoms were reported in a minority.
calculationMethod: cohort-table approximation: 2.5 + 2 × max(severe-pain rate, systemic-effects rate); the maximum is used because overlap was not reported

sources.label: Prospective study of 750 definite spider bites

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort estimate
typicalOutcome: Usually localized effects, with severe pain or minor systemic symptoms in a minority of the qualifying Australian intertidal and house-spider cohort.
calculationMethod: cohort-table approximation: 2.5 + 2 × max(severe-pain rate, systemic-effects rate); the maximum is used because overlap was not reported

sources.label: Prospective study of 750 definite spider bites

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort
typicalOutcome: Usually brief localized pain with possible redness or swelling.

sources.label: Prospective study of 45 definite wolf-spider bites

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort estimate
typicalOutcome: Usually localized, short-lived pain or discomfort; severe pain and systemic symptoms were uncommon in the qualifying Australian jumping-spider cohort.
calculationMethod: cohort-table approximation: 2.5 + 2 × max(severe-pain rate, systemic-effects rate); the maximum is used because overlap was not reported

sources.label: Prospective study of 750 definite spider bites

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort
typicalOutcome: Usually brief localized pain or discomfort; serious effects were not observed in the qualifying cohort.

sources.label: Prospective study of 168 definite huntsman bites

evidenceScope: Australian family-level cohort estimate
typicalOutcome: Localized pain was sometimes severe in the small qualifying Australian ant-spider cohort; minor systemic symptoms occurred in a minority.
calculationMethod: cohort-table approximation: 2.5 + 2 × max(severe-pain rate, systemic-effects rate); the maximum is used because overlap was not reported

sources.label: Prospective study of 750 definite spider bites

evidenceScope: genus fallback derived from historical Sydney funnel-web evidence
typicalOutcome: A minority of bites may cause rapid, life-threatening systemic envenoming.
safetyMessage: Never handle. A suspected funnel-web bite is a medical emergency.

sources.label: Systematic review of recorded funnel-web bites

evidenceScope: genus estimate from 20 verified bites in the United States and Australia
typicalOutcome: Immediate localized pain, often with redness or swelling; pain is sometimes severe but usually self-limited. Minor systemic symptoms occurred in a small minority, and no necrosis occurred in the qualifying cohort.
calculationMethod: fixed grouped-outcome conversion: 10 severe-pain cases at 4.5 and 10 other local-effect cases at 2.5

sources.label: Verified Cheiracanthium bites in the United States and Australia (20 cases)
Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)

evidenceScope: genus estimate from five verified North American bites
typicalOutcome: Minor, self-limited local effects. The verified series did not document serious systemic illness or tissue necrosis.
calculationMethod: all five verified bites mapped to the minor/local category

sources.label: Verified envenomations by crevice weaver spiders (five cases)

evidenceScope: genus estimate supported by prospective cohorts of Lampona cylindrata and Lampona murina
typicalOutcome: Localized pain and redness; a substantial minority of verified bites produced a painful or irritating lesion lasting several days. No necrotic ulcers occurred in the qualifying cohort.
calculationMethod: fixed grouped-outcome conversion from two identified Lampona species

sources.label: Prospective study of 130 definite white-tail bites

evidenceScope: genus fallback derived primarily from redback evidence
typicalOutcome: Potential for prolonged severe pain, muscle cramping, and systemic symptoms.
safetyMessage: A suspected widow-spider bite with severe or spreading symptoms warrants prompt medical advice.

sources.label: Prospective cohort of formally identified redback bites

evidenceScope: genus estimate from 23 definite Steatoda bites
typicalOutcome: Pain is expected and can persist for hours; severe pain and minor systemic symptoms such as nausea, headache, lethargy, or malaise occurred in minorities. Effects were generally less severe than widow-spider envenoming.
safetyMessage: Seek medical advice for severe, spreading, or persistent symptoms after a suspected false-widow bite.
calculationMethod: cohort-table approximation: 2.5 + 2 × max(26% severe pain, 30% systemic effects)

sources.label: Prospective cohort of 28 definite non-widow theridiid bites

evidenceScope: small Oregon species series of five verified bites
typicalOutcome: Localized pain, redness, itching, or swelling, sometimes with mild short-lived symptoms such as headache, nausea, anxiety, or muscle cramping. No serious outcome occurred in the five verified Oregon cases.
calculationMethod: fixed case-pattern conversion from five published verified bites

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)

evidenceScope: historical Sydney funnel-web species complex
typicalOutcome: Most confirmed bites in the least-biased historical sample were local or minor, but a minority caused rapid severe envenoming.
safetyMessage: Never handle. A suspected funnel-web bite is a medical emergency.

sources.label: Systematic review of recorded funnel-web bites

evidenceScope: species-specific average not independently established; a supported genus estimate is available
typicalOutcome: Verified Oregon cases support localized pain, redness, swelling, and occasional minor systemic symptoms, but the available species series is too small for a stable independent average.

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)
Verified Cheiracanthium bites in the United States and Australia (20 cases)

evidenceScope: species estimate from eight verified bites
typicalOutcome: Localized pain, redness, swelling, or itching; pain was sometimes described as severe but was typically brief, with a median duration of about 40 minutes.
calculationMethod: fixed grouped-outcome conversion: five minor/local cases at 2.5 and three severe-local-pain cases at 4.5

sources.label: Verified bites by the woodlouse spider, Dysdera crocata

evidenceScope: species average not established; one verified Oregon bite and a family fallback are available
typicalOutcome: The single verified Oregon hobo-spider bite caused pain, redness, and calf twitching that resolved within about 12 hours; one case cannot establish an average.

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)

evidenceScope: species average not established; one verified Oregon bite and a family fallback are available
typicalOutcome: The single verified Oregon giant-house-spider bite caused local pain, itching, and redness and resolved within about a day; one case cannot establish an average.

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)

evidenceScope: species-specific average not established; a supported Kukulcania genus estimate is available
typicalOutcome: Southern-house-spider bites are represented by the broader crevice-weaver evidence, but the current verified series is too small for a stable independent species average.

sources.label: Verified envenomations by crevice weaver spiders (five cases)

evidenceScope: species
typicalOutcome: Localized pain and redness; some bites produce an irritating lesion lasting several days.

sources.label: Prospective study of 130 definite white-tail bites

evidenceScope: species
typicalOutcome: Localized pain and redness; some bites produce an irritating lesion lasting several days.

sources.label: Prospective study of 130 definite white-tail bites

evidenceScope: species
typicalOutcome: Often prolonged severe pain; systemic effects occur in a meaningful minority.
safetyMessage: A suspected widow-spider bite with severe or spreading symptoms warrants prompt medical advice.

sources.label: Prospective cohort of formally identified redback bites

evidenceScope: species average not established; genus fallback derived primarily from redback evidence
typicalOutcome: Western black-widow bites can cause latrodectism, but the current benchmark lacks a representative all-bite species cohort for an independent average.
safetyMessage: A suspected widow-spider bite with severe or spreading pain, muscle cramping, or systemic symptoms warrants prompt medical advice.

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)
Prospective cohort of formally identified redback bites used for the genus fallback

evidenceScope: species average not established
typicalOutcome: Available evidence is not representative enough to establish the average confirmed-bite outcome.
safetyMessage: A suspected recluse bite with worsening pain, spreading skin changes, fever, or other systemic symptoms warrants medical evaluation.

sources.label: Clinical review of brown-recluse bites
Review of reported brown-recluse diagnoses

evidenceScope: species average not established; only sparse verified jumping-spider evidence is available
typicalOutcome: Available verified jumping-spider cases describe localized pain, itching, redness, or swelling with short recovery, but they are too sparse for a species-specific average.

sources.label: Verified spider bites in Oregon (33 cases)

evidenceScope: species-specific average not established; a supported Steatoda genus estimate is available
typicalOutcome: Cupboard-spider bites are represented within the broader Steatoda evidence, but the available data do not support a stable independent species average.
safetyMessage: Seek medical advice for severe, spreading, or persistent symptoms after a suspected false-widow bite.

sources.label: Prospective cohort of 28 definite non-widow theridiid bites
